flavorText: Beast Masters forge a magical partnership with a primal companion that grows in strength beside them.

features.description: You gain a Primal Beast companion (Land, Sea, or Sky). It obeys your commands, acts on your turn when commanded with a bonus action, or defaults to Initiative count 20 when not commanded. Its statistics scale with your ranger level and proficiency bonus.
Your companion gains improved utility actions and responsiveness to your commands.
Your companion can make additional attacks and strikes with heightened ferocity.
When you cast a spell targeting yourself, it can also affect your beast companion if the beast is within 30 feet.

beast-master-rangers-companion.summary: Fight and travel with a trained companion so the ranger’s battlefield presence becomes truly two-bodied.
beast-master-rangers-companion.description: Ranger’s Companion is the defining Beast Master feature. It should feel like a real partnership with its own action economy and tactical presence, not a cosmetic pet.

beast-master-exceptional-training.summary: Your companion becomes more dependable and responsive as your partnership deepens.
beast-master-exceptional-training.description: Exceptional Training is about coordination. It tells the player this subclass scales by improving the team-up, not by abandoning the beast.

beast-master-bestial-fury.summary: Your companion’s offense ramps up so it remains a meaningful combat contributor at higher levels.
beast-master-bestial-fury.description: Bestial Fury is essential to keeping the companion threatening into later tiers of play.

beast-master-share-spells.summary: Your magic and your companion’s presence intertwine more closely, turning the pair into a stronger unified hunter.
beast-master-share-spells.description: Share Spells is one of the subclass’s late-game rewards for sticking with the companion fantasy. It makes the beast feel like part of the ranger’s magic footprint, not just an extra body.
